docs.title: ExpenseEase — Expense Tracker App
docs.text: ExpenseEase is a cross-platform mobile application that helps individuals manage their personal finances by tracking income and expenses. It provides an ad-free and fully accessible experience, solving the issue of in-app purchase restrictions present in many other expense tracking apps.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
ExpenseEase is a mobile application developed using React Native and Expo with a Node.js and Express backend, connected to a PostgreSQL database(AWS RDS). It enables users to track income and expenses, view total balance, and analyze spending trends through visual charts.

The app includes authentication through JWT and supports two roles: User and Admin. Admins can view registered users, while individual users manage their own transactions.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: ExpenseEase provides users with a simple, free, and secure platform to manage daily financial activities. It includes secure login, transaction tracking, dashboard summaries, and analytics without requiring paid upgrades.

docs.title: Objectives
docs.text:
• Allow users to record income and expenses.
  • Display real-time balance and recent transactions.
  • Provide visual charts for income and expense analysis.
  • Enable CRUD operations on financial entries.
  • Support admin access to view registered users.

docs.title: Authentication Module
docs.text: Test Case No. Module Name Test Case Description Acceptance Criteria TC01 Authentication Register a new user with valid name, email, and password User record created successfully in DB and returns 201 Created TC02 Authentication Attempt to register using an existing email ID API returns 409 Conflict with message "User already exists" TC03 Authentication Register a user without providing a required field (email/password) API returns 400 Bad Request with validation error TC04 Authentication Register with invalid email format API returns 400 Bad Request with message "Invalid email format" TC05 Authentication Login with valid credentials (existing user) Returns 200 OK and "Login successful" message TC06 Authentication Login with invalid password Returns 401 Unauthorized with message "Invalid credentials" TC07 Authentication Login with non-existing email Returns 404 Not Found with message "User not found" TC08 Authentication Attempt login with empty email/password fields Returns 400 Bad Request with validation message TC09 Authentication Check case sensitivity of email during login Login should work regardless of email case (if case-insensitive) TC10 Authentication Logout request from an active user session Returns 200 OK and session is cleared or token invalidated

docs.title: Database Interface
docs.text:
• Service: PostgreSQL
  • Purpose: Stores user and transaction data.

docs.title: Frontend-Backend API Interface
docs.text:
• Protocol: REST (HTTPS)
  • Format: JSON
  • Endpoints: CRUD for transactions, summary fetch, and admin user view.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: ExpenseEase enables users to securely log in, record income and expenses, and monitor their financial status in real time. It also includes an admin role with access to basic user statistics, ensuring visibility without compromising individual data privacy.

docs.title: Core Functionalities
docs.text: Feature Description Signup New users register via JWT authentication. Their details are synced to PostgreSQL. Login Authenticated access for existing users. Dashboard Summary Shows total income, total expenses, and calculated balance dynamically. Create Transaction Add income or expense with title, amount, and notes. View Transactions Display all recent transactions made by the user. Update Transactions Modify an existing transaction’s details. Update Balance Balance recalculates automatically after each add/update/delete. Delete Transaction Remove old or incorrect entries. Pull to Refresh Refresh dashboard data from backend. Charts Visualize income and expense distribution over a selected date range. Logout Securely clear session and redirect to login.

docs.title: 1. Authentication Module
docs.text:
• Handled entirely through JWT.
  • Provides user registration, login, and token-based session management.
  • Syncs user data into the users table on first login.

docs.title: 2. Dashboard Module
docs.text:
• Fetches summarized financial data (income, expense, balance).
  • Displays recent transactions and allows pull-to-refresh.

docs.title: 3. Transaction Module
docs.text:
• Handles creation, update, deletion, and listing of transactions.
  • Updates balance dynamically after every change.

docs.title: 4. Chart Module
docs.text:
• Fetches filtered data from backend (based on date range).
  • Renders visual charts comparing income and expenses.

docs.title: 5. Admin Module
docs.text:
• Restricted to users with the admin role.
  • Allows viewing total registered users and their names for reporting purposes.

docs.title: Roles and Authorization
docs.text:
Role Access Level Description User Standard Can perform all personal expense operations (CRUD). Admin Elevated Can view total number of users and their names via protected admin endpoints.

Authorization is enforced using JWT session verification and role-based access checks on the backend.

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Frontend React Native (Expo) Backend Node.js with Express Database PostgreSQL Authentication JWT Charts React Native Chart Kit Documentation MkDocs

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Term Definition CRUD Create, Read, Update, Delete operations for transactions. Admin User with elevated privileges to view registered users. Dashboard Main screen showing financial summary and recent transactions. Income Money added to balance. Expense Money subtracted from balance. React Native Framework for building cross-platform mobile apps. Expo Toolchain simplifying React Native development and deployment.

docs.title: Technical Documentation — Architecture, DFD, Auth & Authentication and Authorization flow
Schema Design
docs.text:
erDiagram
    users ||--o{ transactions : "has"
    roles ||--o{ users : "assigned to"

    users {
        uuid id PK
        uuid role_id FK "→ roles.id"
        varchar(100) first_name
        varchar(100) last_name
        varchar(255) email
        timestamp created_at
        timestamp updated_at
        timestamp deleted_at
    }

    roles {
        uuid id PK
        varchar(20) name "e.g., 'user', 'admin'"
        timestamp created_at
        timestamp updated_at
        timestamp deleted_at
    }

    transactions {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK "→ users.id"
        varchar(20) entry_type "income / expense"
        varchar(255) title
        decimal amount
        date occurred_at
        timestamp created_at
        timestamp updated_at
        timestamp deleted_at 
    }

docs.title: Authentication and Authorization flow
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant B as Backend (Express)
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    participant SM as Secrets Manager

    U->>B: POST /login (email, password)
    B->>DB: Validate user credentials
    DB-->>B: Return user + role
    B->>SM: Fetch JWT secret
    B-->>U: Return JWT (includes user_id, role)
    U->>B: Access API with JWT
    B->>SM: Verify token using secret
    SM-->>B: Token valid
    B-->>U: Authorized response

docs.title: Screens
docs.text:
1. Signup Screen — User registration via JWT.
  2. Login Screen — User authentication.
  3. Verify Email Screen — Optional email verification.
  4. Home Dashboard — Displays balance summary and recent transactions.
  5. Create Transaction Screen — Add income or expense entries.
  6. Charts Screen — Visualize income vs expense by date range.
  7. Admin Screen — Displays total users and names.

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
• Minimal and clean interface using React Native components.
  • Consistent color scheme for income (green) and expense (red).
  • Pull-to-refresh interaction for data reload.

docs.title: Primary Use Case
docs.text: ExpenseEase enables individuals to record, track, and analyze their income and expenses easily. It provides a comprehensive financial overview — total balance, categorized charts, and transaction history — all without premium restrictions.

docs.title: Target Audience
docs.text:
• Individuals seeking a simple personal finance manager.
  • Students, freelancers, and professionals wanting full access to expense tracking without payment barriers.
  • Users frustrated by commercial apps that hide critical features behind paywalls.

docs.title: App’s USP / Advantages
docs.text:
• Completely Free: No in-app purchases or hidden tiers.
  • Cross-Platform: One app for both Android, iOS and Web.
  • Transparent Role System: Admins can monitor user without invading privacy.
  • Real-Time Insights: Automatic recalculation and live charts.

docs.title: Long-Term Vision
docs.text:
• Add budgeting and category tagging for deeper spending insights.
  • Enable export of reports in PDF and CSV formats.
  • Integrate AI-powered features to extract transaction details from uploaded bill photos and automatically store them in the database.
  • Introduce voice input functionality, allowing users to record expenses verbally for automatic entry.
  • Implement AI-based spending predictions and personalized financial suggestions.
  • Maintain open access, transparency, and scalability as the app grows to support community use.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. User Authentication

  2. User signs up or logs in.

  3. Backend verifies and syncs the user.

  4. Dashboard Initialization

  5. App fetches total income, expense, and recent transactions.

  6. Transaction Operations

  7. User can add, edit, or delete transactions.

  8. Each change updates totals in real time.

  9. Chart Visualization

  10. User selects a custom date range to analyze income vs. expense.

  11. Admin Operations

  12. Admin views registered users through a secure endpoint.

  13. Logout

  14. Session token is cleared; user redirected to login screen.

docs.title: Flow Chart
docs.text:
flowchart TD
A[Start] --> B[User opens app]
B --> C[Sign In]
C --> D{Authentication Successful?}
D -- No --> E[Show Error Message]
D -- Yes --> F[Fetch User Role from DB]

F --> G{Role}
G -- Admin --> H[Redirect to Admin Dashboard]
G -- User --> I[Redirect to User Dashboard]

H --> J[Admin views number of users]
I --> K[User views personal dashboard]
K --> L[User can perform CRUD transactions]
